fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England. The West Indies won the inaugural tournament, defeating Australia in the final.
India won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2011 on home soil, defeating Sri Lanka in the final at Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for most international centuries in cricket history, with 100 centuries across Test and One Day International formats.
Kapil Dev captained India to its first Cricket World Cup title in 1983, defeating the West Indies in the final at Lord's.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
Lionel Messi won his first FIFA World Cup title with Argentina in 2022, defeating France on penalties after a 3-3 draw.
The English Premier League was founded in 1992, replacing the old First Division as the top tier of English football.
Diego Maradona's famous 'Hand of God' goal occurred in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final between Argentina and England.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
Badminton has been part of the Summer Olympics since 1992, when it debuted at the Barcelona Games.
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton, at the 2016 Rio Olympics.
The Uber Cup, first held in 1956-57, is the premier international women's team badminton championship, and is the counterpart to the men's Thomas Cup.
Lin Dan of China is one of the most decorated badminton players in history, having won Olympic gold medals in both 2008 and 2012.
The Wimbledon Championships, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and is played on grass courts.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history.
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Serena Williams won 23 Grand Slam singles titles, the most of any player in the Open Era.
Roger Federer holds the record for most Wimbledon men's singles titles, with 8 championships.
